purpose: Wraps electron-updater into a single-state-machine service that powers the in-app auto-update UI. Subscribes to autoUpdater lifecycle events (checking-for-update, update-available, download-progress, update-downloaded, error), keeps a single shared `state` object, and pushes snapshots to the renderer via the injected `broadcast('update-status', snapshot)` channel. Auto-downloads and auto-installs on quit are enabled. A 10s post-init startup check is scheduled so the user gets an update banner shortly after launch.
gotchas: All update plumbing is no-op when !app.isPackaged — running from source emits a one-shot snapshot then exits initAutoUpdater early, so dev builds will never see real update events.
`initialized` flag prevents re-init but the function silently returns if called twice; passing a fresh broadcastFn second-time-around will be ignored.

quitAndInstallUpdate uses setImmediate then autoUpdater.quitAndInstall(false, true) — the (isSilent=false, isForceRunAfter=true) combo means a visible installer will appear; a silent flow would require different flags.
`startupCheckTimer` is the only timer captured for dispose — if a user-initiated checkForUpdates is in flight at app quit, it is not awaited.
patterns: Module-level singleton state object shared by every event handler; mutation through `setState(patch)` triggers an `emit()` that broadcasts the entire snapshot to the renderer.
Renderer push channel is `update-status`; the snapshot is the source of truth (no incremental diffs).
Lifecycle: initAutoUpdater(broadcast) at app ready -> auto event-driven updates -> disposeAutoUpdater() on quit.
conventions: Snapshots always re-read app.getVersion() so the version field cannot drift.
Errors are caught at every async boundary — checkForUpdates().catch(() => {}) on the timer guarantees no unhandled-rejection at startup.
Human-readable `message` field is set alongside semantic state (checking/available/downloaded/error) for direct UI consumption.
